summary: Refactor PopoverWrapper `onClick` to ignore clicks on the toolbar button

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR enables clicking on popover toolbar buttons to close the popover.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
In the popover wrapper, the `onClick` method was refactored to ignore clicks on the popover toolbar button. This allows clicking the button to actually close the popover. 

Previously, clicking the toolbar button while the popover was open would cause the popover to close and immediately re-open.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

Previous Behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7556715b-792a-44e1-9a23-ae837805b337



PR Behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c46a87be-48f6-4311-81d2-5222aae48a6f



<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 15.6

### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

When using links to non-md files on disk, it would be neat to be able to quickly show/locate such file in the file explorer, grab it and, for example, send it to someone. It could be as non-invasive as an item in context menu after right-clicking the link.

### Example Workflow

I believe that many people uses MarkDown to connect note files with their disk folder structures filled with other-than-text datafiles. Using links to such files enables to maintain more complex structers over the usually insufficient folder hiearchical structure. Editing such files is realy easy with Ctrl+Click, but whenever this filed is need to be moved, sent to someone or deleted, location of the file in the filebrowser (especially with relative file links) is a multistep and error-prone solution so far.

### Caveats

I assume this feature should cause no issues to current features.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: feat: Text transform to remove ligatures

description.text:
Closes #5820

## Description
Adds a text transform to substitute/replace/expand ligatures.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0fe7ab88-85d6-4d24-a33c-f79c6e0dfaf4

## Changes
I added another of the text transforms that we've had a spate of recently.

## Additional information

This is marked as a draft PR only because I think @wideaperture may want to nail down:

* the exact label for the UI menu item; currently it is _Substitute ligatures_
* firmly decide on the exact set of ligatures to be substituted/replaced/expanded
* decide where/how the transform is to be accessed
  * via the existing popup menu
  * a new _Transforms_ submenu of the existing _Edit_ menu
  * both?
  * something else entirely

Purely to facilitate manual testing the transform is actioned by an item in the context menu.

Tested on: Windows 11.

summary: XeLaTeX PDF export doesn't render all images any more

description.text:
### Description

Hi,

Unfortunately, when exporting some documents as PDF files through the XeLaTeX option some images become completely blank.

I tried running a separate version of Pandoc and even reinstalling Zettlr, but nothing worked. I also tried to roll back to version 3.4.4, but that didn't work either. I'd used this version before and it exported my documents fine.

Saving a copy of the affected images through Photoshop seems to work, although it doesn't scale well when some of my documents feature more than 100 images. There seem to be some issues with Pandoc or the PDF engine itself. 

Running a Pandoc command through Windows' Command Prompt gives the same result. Installing a different LaTeX distribution (from MikTeX to TeX Live) also didn't work. Some other engines (e.g. pdflatex) do not even display the second image.

I tried to find the problem by making a new markdown file with two images. The first image isn't visible after the export.

[Test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21997802/Test.zip)

### Reproducing

1. Download the .zip file 
2. Open the .md file
3. File > Export > XeLaTeX PDF
4. Open the PDF

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description

Currently, we can put a list of 'keywords' in the YAML Frontmatter, and find files with these keywords (tags) through the Filter (at the top of the file manager).
I use pandoc to convert markdown files into epub files. Once converted, the list of "keywords" disapear. Howerver a list of "subject" is properly converted into tags for the ebook.
So I prefer to use a list of "subject" instead of "keywords" in the YAML.

### Proposed Changes

Enhancing the "Filter" field above the file manager to take into account a list of "subject" in the YAML Frontmatter. (in the exact same way it works for "keywords)

### Caveats

Might slow down the filtering algorythm, because it would check more possibilities?

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

None

### Your Platform

- [X] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version
Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: `Ctrl-C` (Copy) keyboard shortcut not working on Nightly builds.

description.text:
### Description

`Ctrl-c` frequently does not work to copy text to the clipboard on recent nightly builds. Selecting `Copy` from the right-click context menu or the `Edit` menu both work fine, and pasting with `Ctrl-V` works same as always. The `Ctrl-Alt-c` (Copy as HTML) and `Ctrl-x` (Cut) shortcuts continue to work as expected.

As you can see in this video, no console output is shown during the glitch:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ea99016-3aa3-450a-a80a-8dad43add409

At least one other user on Discord reports the same issue and additionally notes:

> select-by-cursor + y is not working in vi mode, when it should, _however_  visual mode + y works as expected.

### Reproducing

On a recent nightly build, select text in the editor and attempt to copy it to the clipboard using the `Ctrl-c` keyboard shortcut. Paste the clipboard contents. Rather than the expected text, the clipboard paste is either empty or contains text copied prior to the most recent attempt.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0-nightly (from August 18, 2025)

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

More discussion and screengrab videos are to be found in the \#help Discord channel ca. August 20th.

summary: Add configurable syntax highlighting aliases for code blocks

description.text: ### Description ### Description Add a configuration option to define syntax highlighting aliases for code blocks. This allows custom code block identifiers (e.g., `python-eval`, `pyx`, `p`) to inherit the syntax highlighting of existing languages. **Motivation:** Enables custom processing pipelines (e.g., via external filters) while preserving editor highlighting, improving readability and workflow efficiency. Users can define shortcuts or meaningful labels without sacrificing syntax highlighting. I am making such a filter, and while i can get away with adding extra classes to the header to achieve the same result, it gets way too verbose very quickly. ### Proposed Changes ### Proposed Changes Add a new configuration section (e.g., in `Editor` or `Advanced` settings) for syntax highlighting aliases. This should accept a key-value pair list where: - **Key**: The custom language identifier (e.g., `python-eval`, `pyx`, `jsx`) - **Value**: The existing language mode to map to (e.g., `python`, `javascript`) **Implementation specifics:** - The alias resolution should occur dynamically during editor rendering. - Only affect syntax highlighting in the editor; should not interfere with export processing or existing code block logic. - Ideally, support both GUI (settings panel input) and config file (e.g., `config.json`) methods. ### Caveats ### Caveats - **Namespace Conflicts:** Custom aliases (e.g., `pyx`) might unintentionally shadow future officially supported language modes. - **Configuration Complexity:** Adding too many aliases could make the settings UI cluttered or confusing for users who don’t need this feature. - **Export Compatibility:** While the change is editor-focused, some export formats (e.g., Pandoc, LaTeX) might misinterpret non-standard language identifiers unless explicitly handled. - **Performance:** Dynamic alias resolution should be lightweight to avoid slowing down editor rendering for documents with many code blocks. ### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself? No ### Zettlr Version None ### Your Platform - [x] Windows - [ ] macOS - [ ] Linux ### Operating System Version Windows 11 ### Additional Information A mockup screen: _No response_

summary: File ordering not refreshed when updating title/heading

description.text:
### Description

I have set the file tree to be ordered by title. But when i update the title field and press save, then the title in the tree is correctly updated, but the ordering in the tree is not. 

### Reproducing

* set file order mode to title
* change the title in the frontmatter of a file
* ctrl+s


### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Introduce a user-defined “Custom Variables” feature, allowing users to define and reuse individual variables across multiple snippets, templates, and documents without manually editing each occurrence.

Currently, Zettlr offers built-in snippet variables such as `${CURRENT_DATE}` or `${CURRENT_ID}`, which can be expanded dynamically. However, there is no way for users to define their own custom variables that can be reused across snippets and templates.

This limitation leads to unnecessary manual edits: for example, if a snippet includes the author’s name, institution, or frequently reused text, users must either
	•	hardcode the text in every snippet (making global changes time-consuming), or
	•	insert placeholders like `${2:Enter your name}` and replace them manually each time.

**Proposed solution:**

Introduce a Custom Variables Table, similar to the autocorrect table, accessible in Zettlr’s preferences.
	•	Users could define variables like `$NAME`, `$LONGNAME`, `$SHORTNAME`, `$INSTITUTION`, `$EMAIL` etc.
	•	These variables would be available in all snippets and templates, just like built-in variables.
	•	Updating a variable’s value would automatically update its expansion everywhere it is used (at the moment of snippet/template expansion).

**Benefits:**
	1.	Consistency – Ensure the same information is used uniformly across multiple snippets, templates, and exported documents.
	2.	Efficiency – Change a frequently reused value (e.g., author name) once, instead of editing every snippet/template that contains it.
	3.	Flexibility – Adapt snippets for different contexts (e.g., work vs. academic) by switching variable sets.
	4.	Familiarity – Align with similar features in other writing tools like Scrivener (`$FullName`, `$AbbrevName`, etc.), easing onboarding for new users.

**Impact:**
This feature would make snippet and template management significantly more scalable, particularly for users maintaining large snippet collections or sharing template packs with others.


### Example Workflow

1.	In Zettlr Preferences → Custom Variables, user defines:
	•	`$NAME` → “Helga Musterfrau”
	•	`$EMAIL` → “helga@somemail.net”
2.	In a snippet template, the user writes:

```YAML
---
title: "${1:Enter a Title}"
author: $NAME
email: $EMAIL
---
```

3.	When inserting the snippet, Zettlr automatically replaces `$NAME` and `$EMAIL` with the values defined in the preferences.
4.	If the user changes `$NAME` in the Custom Variables Table to “Dr. Helga Musterfrau”, all future snippet expansions will reflect that without editing the snippet itself.


### Caveats

	•	**Scope of replacement**: Variables should expand only at insertion/compilation time, not alter already written text unless explicitly re-run.
	•	**Conflicts**: Variable names must not clash with built-in snippet variables (`$CURRENT_DATE`, `$CURRENT_ID`, etc.).
	•	**Portability**: Shared snippets relying on custom variables might require a warning or export/import of the variable set to preserve functionality.
	•	**UI consideration**: Needs a simple, intuitive way to add/edit/delete variables, ideally in a table similar to the autocorrect interface.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

Of course, this feature falls firmly into the “nice-to-have” category. However, I believe it would add a layer of flexibility to Zettlr that would benefit not only dedicated power users, but also the wider user base.
